modules.title: AWS Security
modules.summary: Practice command-line AWS inspection and small, bounded security remediations.

labs.title: AWS Security Basics
labs.summary: Use the AWS CLI to bring assigned resources to a clearly stated security baseline.
labs.prompt: You are working in a dedicated AWS lab account with short-lived credentials. Start in ~/work/aws: read README.md for the full target state and use resource-map.env (loaded automatically in new shells) for assigned bucket, security group, launch template, trail, and SNS topic names/IDs. Work in order: (1) describe/get each assigned resource and note the starting state; (2) apply remediations; (3) re-describe to confirm; (4) update findings.md with assigned IDs and your verified final checklist state. Bring only those assigned resources to this final state: the research bucket has versioning enabled and all four public-access-block controls enabled; the security group no longer allows public SSH from 0.0.0.0/0 but keeps internal HTTPS from 10.44.0.0/16; the default launch template version does not assign a public IP, requires IMDSv2 tokens, and uses encrypted root storage; the CloudTrail trail has log file validation enabled; create the lab's root-account CloudWatch alarm (OpsCoach-<session>-RootAccountUsage per README and templates/root-account-alarm.json — this lab uses a convention metric namespace, not a full CloudTrail metric-filter pipeline) and route it to the assigned SNS topic. Use starter request bodies in templates/ when you need dense AWS JSON. In findings.md, checklist rows must reflect post-remediation state you verified — not copied placeholders.
hints: Confirm assigned names with resource-map.env and README.md before changing anything; use get/describe first.
Edit templates in templates/ for dense JSON (public access block, revoke-public-ssh-rule.json for revoke-security-group-ingress, launch template data, root-account alarm).
findings.md shows format examples only — replace each checklist row with the state you verified after remediation.
After launch template changes, set the corrected version as default; list assigned resource IDs/names, alarm topic, and final checklist state in findings.md.
